req.title: System-Admin updates policy-document
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a policy-document through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that an authenticated system-admin can update a policy-document via REST API, with mandatory field validation, audit-record creation, and correct HTTP status codes. I derived five test cases: (1) EP positive – valid admin updates a document and gets 200/201 + audit record; (2) NEG – unauthenticated request returns 401; (3) NEG/DT – forbidden role (non-admin) returns 403; (4) BVA – mandatory field 'title' submitted as empty string triggers 400; (5) STC – document transitions from draft to published state and audit record reflects new state. Each test is self-contained via fixtures and helper placeholders, stays under 40 lines, and is idempotent through setup/teardown helpers.

tests.name: test_admin_updates_policy_document_success
preconditions: A policy-document with known ID exists
actions: Create or retrieve a policy-document via <seed_policy_document>
Send PUT /api/policy-documents/{id} with all mandatory fields populated
Query audit log via <get_latest_audit_record> for the document ID
expected: Response body contains the entity id
Audit record exists referencing the updated document id
trace: R-WEB-054: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_admin_updates_policy_document_success(
    admin_token,        # <fixture> returns valid Bearer token for role:admin
    seed_policy_document,  # <fixture> creates doc, yields id, deletes on teardown
    get_latest_audit_record,  # <helper_fn>(doc_id) -> audit dict or None
    base_url,           # <fixture> returns API base URL string
):
    doc_id = seed_policy_document
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {
        "title": "Updated Policy Title",
        "content": "Updated content body.",
        "effective_date": "2025-01-01",
        "status": "active"
    }
    response = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/api/policy-documents/{doc_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    assert str(doc_id) in str(body.get("id", body.get("data", {}).get("id", "")))
    audit = get_latest_audit_record(doc_id)
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record not found after update"
    assert audit.get("entity_id") == str(doc_id)

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401
preconditions: A policy-document with known ID exists
actions: Send PUT /api/policy-documents/{id} with no Authorization header
expected: Response body contains an authentication error message
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401(
    seed_policy_document,  # <fixture> creates doc, yields id, deletes on teardown
    base_url,              # <fixture> returns API base URL string
):
    doc_id = seed_policy_document
    payload = {
        "title": "Should Not Update",
        "content": "Irrelevant content.",
        "effective_date": "2025-01-01",
        "status": "active"
    }
    response = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/api/policy-documents/{doc_id}",
        json=payload,
        # Intentionally omitting Authorization header
    )
    assert response.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got: {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert any(kw in error_text for kw in ("unauthorized", "unauthenticated", "authentication", "token"))

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated but holds role:viewer (non-admin)
A policy-document with known ID exists
actions: Send PUT /api/policy-documents/{id} with all mandatory fields populated
expected: Response body contains a forbidden/permission error message
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_forbidden_role_returns_403(
    viewer_token,          # <fixture> returns valid Bearer token for role:viewer
    seed_policy_document,  # <fixture> creates doc, yields id, deletes on teardown
    base_url,              # <fixture> returns API base URL string
):
    doc_id = seed_policy_document
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {viewer_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {
        "title": "Forbidden Update Attempt",
        "content": "Should be rejected.",
        "effective_date": "2025-01-01",
        "status": "active"
    }
    response = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/api/policy-documents/{doc_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert response.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got: {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert any(kw in error_text for kw in ("forbidden", "permission", "access denied", "not authorized"))

tests.name: test_empty_title_boundary_returns_400
preconditions: A policy-document with known ID exists
actions: Send PUT /api/policy-documents/{id} with title set to empty string (boundary: length=0)
expected: Response body references the 'title' field as invalid
trace: R-WEB-054: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_empty_title_boundary_returns_400(
    admin_token,           # <fixture> returns valid Bearer token for role:admin
    seed_policy_document,  # <fixture> creates doc, yields id, deletes on teardown
    base_url,              # <fixture> returns API base URL string
):
    doc_id = seed_policy_document
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {
        "title": "",       # BVA: boundary at length 0 (below minimum valid length)
        "content": "Some valid content.",
        "effective_date": "2025-01-01",
        "status": "active"
    }
    response = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/api/policy-documents/{doc_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert response.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got: {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "title" in error_text, "Error response should reference the 'title' field"

tests.name: test_draft_to_published_state_transition_and_audit
actions: Create a policy-document in 'draft' state via <seed_policy_document_draft>
Query audit log via <get_latest_audit_record> for the document ID
expected: Audit record captures state transition from 'draft' to 'published'
trace: R-WEB-054: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_draft_to_published_state_transition_and_audit(
    admin_token,                 # <fixture> returns valid Bearer token for role:admin
    seed_policy_document_draft,  # <fixture> creates doc in 'draft' state, yields id
    get_latest_audit_record,     # <helper_fn>(doc_id) -> audit dict or None
    base_url,                    # <fixture> returns API base URL string
):
    doc_id = seed_policy_document_draft
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {
        "title": "Final Published Policy",
        "content": "Approved and published content.",
        "effective_date": "2025-06-01",
        "status": "published"   # STC: transition draft -> published
    }
    response = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/api/policy-documents/{doc_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    result_status = body.get("status") or body.get("data", {}).get("status", "")
    assert result_status == "published", f"Expected 'published', got: {result_status!r}"
    audit = get_latest_audit_record(doc_id)
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record not found after state transition"
    assert audit.get("new_state") == "published" or audit.get("status") == "published"
